figures.caption: Figure 2: The diagram of radio frequency circuit. The fingerprint of an emitter are mainly from non-ideal characteristics of individual modules.

Figure 3: Overview of the proposed robust RFFI framework, integrating the proposed multi-scale Transformer architecture, called SinFormer and the two-stage training method to achieve robust and adaptable RFFI models.
Figure 4: The scenario of RFFI consists of a training stage and a testing stage.

Figure 5: Figure of the impacts of RF fingerprint features (a) CFO, (b) amplifier nonlinear effects on signals. In each subplot, the top and middle sections show the time-domain signals after being influenced by different RF fingerprint features on the same signal. The bottom section represents the squared differences of the signal amplitudes at corresponding sampling points, highlighting the time-domain discrepancies between the two signals.

Figure 6: The architecture of proposed SinFormer for RFFI. Top is the overall flow from the input signal to the output emitter class probabilities. Bottom-left focuses on the Inception Self-attention module, which enables both large-scale and small-scale feature interaction. Bottom-right depicts the Down-sampling Attention mechanism, which facilitates targeted scale-specific feature interaction.
Figure 7: The processing flow of proposed down-sampling attention module.
Figure 8: The overview of the proposed unsupervised general feature learning approach, MAE-AC.
Table 1: The details of the SinFormer model
Table 2: The computational efficiency of the models
Table 4: Classification accuracy (%) of comparative experiments

Figure 9: The t-SNE visualizations of extracted unseen session Out-2 signal feature distribution for different methods trained on In-1 to In-7 sessions, when training data is sufficient. Features with the same emitter label are plotted in the same color.
Table 6: Results (%) of experiments for open-set recognition
Figure 10: Histograms of known and unknown emitter scores for each method using the open-set recognition approach ViM.
Figure 11: Performance results of various methods across SNR ranges from -20 dB to 20 dB.
Figure 12: Accuracy vs. SIR in other practical scenarios.
Figure 13: Impact of hyper-parameter variations on results on In-1 to In-7, In-8, In-9, Out-1, and Out-2 Sessions
Figure 14: The scalability of proposed method
